provenance_defaults.source: self-authored (multi-agent authored + adversarially verified; AU/AMH context; corroborate against AMH/TGA at review). NOT derived from LITFL notes.
provenance_defaults.authored_by: claude-fable-5 workflow (author+verify agents; draft — KL to attest)

records.mechanism_of_action: Competitively and reversibly inhibits intestinal brush-border alpha-glucosidase enzymes (and pancreatic alpha-amylase), delaying hydrolysis of complex carbohydrates and disaccharides to absorbable monosaccharides, thereby blunting postprandial glucose rise
records.effect: Reduced postprandial hyperglycaemia

records.mechanism_of_action: Long-acting competitive antagonist at airway muscarinic receptors (with kinetic selectivity for M3), blocking acetylcholine-mediated bronchoconstriction of airway smooth muscle
records.effect: Sustained bronchodilation

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective agonist at beta-2 adrenoceptors, activating adenylate cyclase to raise cyclic AMP, relaxing airway smooth muscle
records.effect: Bronchodilation with rapid onset and prolonged duration

records.mechanism_of_action: Third-generation retinoid that binds retinoic acid nuclear receptors (RAR-beta and RAR-gamma), normalising keratinocyte differentiation and proliferation to reduce microcomedone formation, with anti-inflammatory activity
records.effect: Comedolytic and anti-inflammatory

records.mechanism_of_action: Oxidising agent that decomposes in skin to release reactive oxygen/free radicals, producing bactericidal activity against Cutibacterium acnes; also keratolytic and mildly comedolytic
records.effect: Antibacterial and keratolytic

records.mechanism_of_action: Nitrogen-containing bisphosphonate that binds hydroxyapatite and, once internalised by osteoclasts, inhibits farnesyl pyrophosphate synthase in the mevalonate pathway, impairing osteoclast function and survival
records.effect: Inhibition of osteoclast-mediated bone resorption, increasing bone mineral density

records.mechanism_of_action: Competitively inhibits HMG-CoA reductase, the rate-limiting enzyme of hepatic cholesterol synthesis, increasing hepatic LDL-receptor expression and clearance of circulating LDL
records.effect: Lowered LDL-cholesterol and triglycerides, modest HDL increase

records.mechanism_of_action: Agonist at spinal GABA-B receptors; hyperpolarises motor neurons and inhibits mono- and poly-synaptic reflex transmission, reducing release of excitatory neurotransmitters
records.effect: Reduction of skeletal muscle spasticity and flexor/extensor spasms

records.mechanism_of_action: Azo prodrug cleaved by colonic bacterial azoreductase to release mesalazine (5-aminosalicylic acid) topically in the colon, which inhibits cyclooxygenase and lipoxygenase pathways, reduces prostaglandin and leukotriene synthesis and scavenges reactive oxygen species
records.effect: Reduced colonic mucosal inflammation

records.mechanism_of_action: Prodrug (beclometasone dipropionate) hydrolysed to active beclometasone-17-monopropionate, which binds the glucocorticoid receptor to suppress airway inflammatory gene transcription and reduce mucosal inflammation
records.effect: Reduced airway inflammation and bronchial hyper-reactivity

records.mechanism_of_action: Long-acting muscarinic antagonist; competitively blocks acetylcholine at M3 muscarinic receptors on airway smooth muscle, inhibiting cholinergic bronchoconstriction.
records.effect: Bronchodilation via reduced cholinergic tone

records.mechanism_of_action: Dissociates to provide bicarbonate ions that buffer hydrogen ions, raising plasma and urinary pH; in the stomach it neutralises gastric acid
records.drug_class: Systemic and urinary alkalinising agent / antacid
records.target: Extracellular hydrogen ion / bicarbonate buffer system
records.effect: Increased plasma and urinary pH; gastric acid neutralisation

records.mechanism_of_action: Synthetic prostamide (prostaglandin F2-alpha analogue) that increases aqueous humour outflow via the uveoscleral and trabecular routes, lowering intraocular pressure
records.effect: Reduced intraocular pressure; increased uveoscleral outflow

records.mechanism_of_action: Non-selective beta-adrenergic receptor antagonist; blocks beta-receptors on the ciliary epithelium reducing aqueous humour production
records.effect: Reduced aqueous humour formation and lowered intraocular pressure

records.mechanism_of_action: Diphenylmethane stimulant laxative; after local hydrolysis to its active metabolite it directly stimulates the colonic mucosa and myenteric plexus, increasing peristalsis and promoting fluid/electrolyte accumulation in the colonic lumen
records.target: Colonic mucosal sensory nerves / enteric plexus
records.effect: Increased colonic motility and intraluminal fluid; laxation

records.mechanism_of_action: Vitamin D3 prohormone hydroxylated in liver to 25-hydroxyvitamin D then in kidney to active calcitriol, which activates the vitamin D receptor to promote intestinal calcium and phosphate absorption and normal bone mineralisation
records.effect: Increased intestinal calcium/phosphate absorption; supports mineralisation and PTH regulation

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective antagonist of post-synaptic alpha-1 adrenoceptors in prostatic, bladder neck and urethral smooth muscle, reducing dynamic bladder outlet obstruction
records.effect: Smooth muscle relaxation and improved urinary flow

records.mechanism_of_action: Purine analogue that, with its active metabolite oxypurinol, inhibits xanthine oxidase, blocking conversion of hypoxanthine to xanthine to uric acid
records.effect: Reduced uric acid production and lowered serum urate

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective inhibitor of dipeptidyl peptidase-4 (DPP-4), preventing degradation of incretin hormones GLP-1 and GIP, thereby enhancing glucose-dependent insulin secretion and suppressing glucagon
records.effect: Glucose-dependent increase in insulin and reduced glucagon, lowering blood glucose

records.mechanism_of_action: NMDA receptor antagonism with enhancement of presynaptic dopamine release and inhibition of dopamine reuptake, plus weak anticholinergic activity; antiviral effect via blockade of the influenza A M2 proton channel preventing viral uncoating
records.target: NMDA receptor; dopamine transporter; influenza A M2 ion channel
records.effect: Reduced parkinsonian rigidity and dyskinesia; inhibition of influenza A replication

records.mechanism_of_action: Blocks epithelial sodium channels (ENaC) in the distal tubule and collecting duct, reducing sodium reabsorption and secondarily reducing potassium and hydrogen ion secretion
records.effect: Mild natriuresis with potassium and magnesium conservation

records.mechanism_of_action: Inhibits transmembrane calcium influx through L-type voltage-gated calcium channels, preferentially in vascular smooth muscle
records.effect: Peripheral and coronary arterial vasodilation, reduced peripheral vascular resistance

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective antagonist of the angiotensin II type 1 (AT1) receptor, blocking angiotensin II-mediated vasoconstriction and aldosterone release
records.effect: Vasodilation and reduced blood pressure

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective competitive antagonism of beta-1 adrenoceptors, reducing sympathetic drive to the heart: lowers heart rate, myocardial contractility and AV nodal conduction, and suppresses renin release
records.effect: Reduced heart rate, cardiac output, blood pressure and myocardial oxygen demand

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective alpha-2 adrenergic receptor agonism reduces aqueous humour production and increases uveoscleral outflow; topically on skin causes vasoconstriction of cutaneous vessels
records.effect: Lowered intraocular pressure; cutaneous vasoconstriction reducing facial erythema

records.mechanism_of_action: Reversible inhibition of carbonic anhydrase II in the ciliary processes, reducing bicarbonate formation and thereby aqueous humour production and intraocular pressure
records.effect: Reduced aqueous humour secretion and lowered intraocular pressure

records.mechanism_of_action: Reversibly and selectively inhibits the aromatase enzyme, blocking peripheral conversion of androgens to oestrogens and lowering circulating oestradiol
records.effect: Systemic oestrogen deprivation

records.mechanism_of_action: Directly, selectively and reversibly inhibits the active site of both free and clot-bound factor Xa, reducing thrombin generation and fibrin clot formation
records.effect: Anticoagulation

records.mechanism_of_action: Directly stimulates postsynaptic dopamine D1 and D2 receptors in the striatum, restoring dopaminergic motor function (no opioid activity despite the name)
records.effect: Rapid reduction of Parkinsonian 'off' time and rigidity

records.mechanism_of_action: Selectively and competitively blocks cardiac beta-1 adrenoceptors, reducing heart rate, contractility, AV nodal conduction and renin release; no intrinsic sympathomimetic activity
records.effect: Reduced heart rate, blood pressure and myocardial oxygen demand

records.mechanism_of_action: Agonism at pituitary dopamine D2 receptors on lactotrophs inhibits prolactin secretion; central dopaminergic agonism supplements striatal dopamine in Parkinson disease
records.effect: Suppression of prolactin release; dopaminergic stimulation

records.mechanism_of_action: Binds the intracellular glucocorticoid receptor, modulating transcription to suppress inflammatory mediators, cytokines and inflammatory cell activity; high topical potency with extensive first-pass inactivation
records.effect: Local anti-inflammatory effect on airway or gut mucosa

records.mechanism_of_action: Long-acting selective dopamine D2 receptor agonist; stimulates D2 receptors on pituitary lactotroph cells to inhibit prolactin synthesis and secretion
records.effect: Sustained suppression of prolactin secretion

records.mechanism_of_action: Synthetic vitamin D3 analogue that binds the vitamin D receptor in keratinocytes, inhibiting proliferation and promoting terminal differentiation
records.effect: Normalises epidermal keratinocyte turnover in psoriatic plaques

records.mechanism_of_action: Potent glucocorticoid that binds the intracellular glucocorticoid receptor, modulating gene transcription to suppress inflammatory mediators and cytokines
records.effect: Anti-inflammatory, immunosuppressive, antiproliferative and vasoconstrictive action in skin

records.mechanism_of_action: Biologically active form of vitamin D (1,25-dihydroxycholecalciferol); binds the vitamin D receptor to increase intestinal absorption of calcium and phosphate, promote bone mineral mobilisation, and suppress parathyroid hormone
records.effect: Raises serum calcium and phosphate; lowers PTH

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective angiotensin II type 1 (AT1) receptor antagonist; blocks angiotensin II-mediated vasoconstriction and aldosterone release, reducing peripheral vascular resistance
records.effect: Vasodilation, reduced blood pressure and cardiac afterload

records.mechanism_of_action: Prodrug converted to methimazole (thiamazole), which inhibits thyroid peroxidase, blocking iodination and coupling of tyrosine residues and thus thyroid hormone synthesis
records.effect: Reduced synthesis of T3 and T4

records.mechanism_of_action: High-molecular-weight cross-linked polyacrylic acid polymer forming a mucoadhesive viscous film that lubricates the ocular surface and prolongs tear-film retention
records.target: Ocular surface tear film (no receptor target)
records.effect: Tear-film stabilisation and relief of dry-eye symptoms

records.mechanism_of_action: Hydrophilic cellulose-derivative polymer acting as a demulcent that coats, hydrates and lubricates the ocular surface, stabilising the tear film
records.target: Ocular surface tear film (no receptor target)
records.effect: Relief of dry-eye symptoms and tear-film stabilisation

records.mechanism_of_action: Non-selective beta-adrenoceptor antagonist with concurrent alpha-1 adrenoceptor blockade; also has antioxidant properties
records.drug_class: Non-selective beta-blocker with alpha-1 blocking activity
records.effect: Reduces heart rate, myocardial contractility and afterload; lowers blood pressure and improves ventricular remodelling in heart failure

records.mechanism_of_action: Inhibits the sodium-chloride cotransporter in the distal convoluted tubule, increasing sodium and water excretion
records.target: Na-Cl cotransporter (NCC) in the distal convoluted tubule
records.effect: Natriuresis and diuresis with sustained reduction in blood pressure and reduced plasma/extracellular volume

records.mechanism_of_action: Prodrug hydrolysed by airway esterases to the active metabolite des-ciclesonide, a glucocorticoid receptor agonist that suppresses inflammatory gene transcription
records.effect: Local anti-inflammatory action reducing airway/nasal mucosal inflammation and bronchial hyper-responsiveness

records.mechanism_of_action: Binds cyclophilin; the complex inhibits calcineurin, blocking dephosphorylation of NFAT and transcription of interleukin-2, thereby suppressing T-lymphocyte activation
records.effect: Selective, reversible suppression of T-cell-mediated immune responses

records.mechanism_of_action: Selectively inhibits presynaptic reuptake of serotonin by blocking the serotonin transporter, increasing synaptic serotonin availability
records.effect: Enhanced serotonergic neurotransmission with antidepressant and anxiolytic effect

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective reversible inhibition of sodium-glucose cotransporter 2 (SGLT2) in the renal proximal convoluted tubule, reducing renal glucose reabsorption and increasing urinary glucose excretion; also promotes natriuresis and osmotic diuresis
records.effect: Glucosuria with insulin-independent lowering of blood glucose, modest weight loss, blood pressure reduction, and cardio-renal protection

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective, competitive inhibition of dipeptidyl peptidase-4 (DPP-4), preventing degradation of incretin hormones (GLP-1, GIP); raised active incretin levels increase glucose-dependent insulin secretion and suppress glucagon release
records.effect: Glucose-dependent lowering of blood glucose with low intrinsic hypoglycaemia risk

records.mechanism_of_action: Synthetic structural analogue of arginine vasopressin acting as a selective agonist at renal V2 receptors, increasing water permeability of collecting-duct principal cells; also stimulates release of factor VIII and von Willebrand factor from endothelium
records.effect: Antidiuresis (increased water reabsorption, concentrated urine) and transient haemostatic effect

records.mechanism_of_action: Inhibition of presynaptic reuptake of serotonin and noradrenaline via the serotonin (SERT) and noradrenaline (NET) transporters, increasing synaptic monoamine availability; desvenlafaxine is the major active metabolite of venlafaxine
records.effect: Antidepressant effect; dose-related increases in noradrenergic tone

records.mechanism_of_action: Binds the cytoplasmic glucocorticoid receptor, and the activated complex modulates transcription of anti-inflammatory and pro-inflammatory genes, suppressing cytokine production, leucocyte migration and immune activation; negligible mineralocorticoid activity
records.effect: Potent anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive effect

records.mechanism_of_action: High-molecular-weight branched polysaccharide acting as an ocular demulcent that retains water on the ocular surface and stabilises the tear film
records.effect: Lubrication and hydration of the ocular surface, relief of dry-eye irritation

records.mechanism_of_action: Water-soluble cellulose polymer (hydroxypropyl methylcellulose) that increases tear-film viscosity and lubricates the ocular surface, prolonging tear contact time and reducing surface friction
records.effect: Symptomatic relief of dry-eye and ocular surface protection

records.mechanism_of_action: Prodrug converted by 11-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type 1 to cortisol, which binds the intracellular glucocorticoid receptor and modulates transcription of anti-inflammatory and metabolic genes
records.effect: Anti-inflammatory, immunosuppressive and glucocorticoid-replacement effects

records.mechanism_of_action: Competitive antagonist at the androgen receptor with progestogenic antigonadotrophic activity that suppresses LH/FSH and thereby testosterone production
records.effect: Antiandrogenic; reduces androgen-dependent tissue stimulation and libido

records.mechanism_of_action: Dabigatran etexilate prodrug is hydrolysed by esterases to dabigatran, a direct competitive inhibitor of free and clot-bound thrombin (factor IIa), preventing conversion of fibrinogen to fibrin
records.effect: Anticoagulation

records.mechanism_of_action: Acts locally in the rectum as a peptising/sequestering agent, chelating cations and displacing bound water within the faecal mass to soften and disperse hardened stool.
records.effect: Stool softening and mobilisation

records.mechanism_of_action: Anionic surface-active wetting agent that lowers the surface tension of the faecal mass, allowing water and lipids to penetrate and soften impacted stool.
records.effect: Increased faecal water penetration and softening

records.mechanism_of_action: Non-absorbed polyol that exerts an osmotic effect in the rectum/distal bowel, drawing water into the lumen to increase stool water content and stimulate reflex evacuation.
records.effect: Osmotic hydration of stool and evacuation

records.mechanism_of_action: Central alpha-2 adrenergic (and imidazoline-I1 receptor) agonist that reduces sympathetic outflow from the CNS, lowering peripheral vascular resistance, heart rate and plasma catecholamines.
records.target: Central alpha-2 adrenoceptors / imidazoline I1 receptors
records.effect: Reduced central sympathetic tone; fall in blood pressure and heart rate

records.mechanism_of_action: Non-absorbed anion-exchange resin that binds bile acids in the intestinal lumen and prevents their enterohepatic reabsorption; hepatic conversion of cholesterol to bile acids increases, upregulating LDL receptors and lowering circulating LDL cholesterol.
records.target: Intestinal bile acids / hepatic LDL receptor upregulation
records.effect: Bile acid depletion and reduced LDL cholesterol

records.mechanism_of_action: Blocks L-type voltage-gated calcium channels in cardiac and vascular smooth muscle, reducing calcium influx
records.effect: Coronary and peripheral vasodilation, negative chronotropy and slowed AV nodal conduction

records.mechanism_of_action: Inhibits carbonic anhydrase II in the ciliary processes, reducing bicarbonate formation and aqueous humour production
records.effect: Reduced aqueous humour secretion and lowered intraocular pressure

records.mechanism_of_action: Inhibits reuptake of serotonin and noradrenaline by blocking the serotonin (SERT) and noradrenaline (NET) transporters
records.effect: Increased synaptic serotonin and noradrenaline; antidepressant, anxiolytic and analgesic effects

records.mechanism_of_action: Inhibits both type 1 and type 2 isoenzymes of 5-alpha-reductase, blocking conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT)
records.effect: Reduces serum and intraprostatic DHT, decreasing prostate volume over months

records.mechanism_of_action: Selectively antagonises alpha-1A (and alpha-1D) adrenoceptors in prostatic and bladder-neck smooth muscle, reducing outflow resistance
records.effect: Prostatic and bladder-neck smooth muscle relaxation

records.mechanism_of_action: Inhibits sodium-glucose co-transporter 2 (SGLT2) in the proximal renal tubule, reducing renal glucose reabsorption
records.effect: Increased urinary glucose excretion with osmotic diuresis and natriuresis; insulin-independent glucose lowering plus cardio-renal benefit

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective, reversible inhibitor of dipeptidyl peptidase-4 (DPP-4); prevents degradation of incretin hormones GLP-1 and GIP, enhancing glucose-dependent insulin secretion and suppressing glucagon.
records.effect: Glucose-dependent lowering of blood glucose with low intrinsic hypoglycaemia risk

records.mechanism_of_action: Prodrug hydrolysed to the active diacid enalaprilat, which competitively inhibits angiotensin-converting enzyme, reducing angiotensin II formation and aldosterone secretion and decreasing bradykinin degradation
records.effect: Vasodilation, natriuresis and reduced afterload/preload

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective, reversible inhibitor of peripheral catechol-O-methyltransferase (COMT); reduces 3-O-methylation of levodopa, decreasing formation of 3-O-methyldopa and prolonging plasma levodopa half-life and CNS availability.
records.effect: Prolongs and smooths levodopa effect, reducing end-of-dose wearing-off

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective mineralocorticoid receptor antagonist that competitively blocks aldosterone binding, reducing sodium reabsorption and potassium excretion in the distal nephron and attenuating aldosterone-mediated cardiovascular fibrosis
records.effect: Natriuresis with potassium retention, lowered blood pressure and reduced cardiac remodelling; low affinity for androgen/progesterone receptors (less gynaecomastia than spironolactone)

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective competitive antagonism of the angiotensin II type 1 (AT1) receptor, blocking angiotensin II-mediated vasoconstriction and aldosterone release
records.effect: Vasodilation, reduced aldosterone secretion and lowered blood pressure

records.mechanism_of_action: Selectively inhibits the intestinal Niemann-Pick C1-like 1 (NPC1L1) sterol transporter at the enterocyte brush border, blocking absorption of dietary and biliary cholesterol; this lowers hepatic cholesterol delivery and upregulates LDL receptors
records.effect: Reduced intestinal cholesterol absorption and lowered LDL-cholesterol

records.mechanism_of_action: Prodrug hydrolysed to an active beta-hydroxyacid that competitively inhibits HMG-CoA reductase, reducing hepatic cholesterol synthesis and upregulating LDL receptors
records.effect: Reduction of LDL cholesterol and total cholesterol

records.mechanism_of_action: Competitively and reversibly antagonises histamine H2 receptors on gastric parietal cells, reducing basal, nocturnal and stimulated gastric acid secretion
records.effect: Reduced gastric acid secretion and raised intragastric pH

records.mechanism_of_action: Potent non-purine selective inhibitor of both the oxidised and reduced forms of xanthine oxidase, blocking conversion of hypoxanthine and xanthine to uric acid
records.effect: Reduced uric acid production and lowered serum urate

records.mechanism_of_action: Blocks L-type voltage-gated calcium channels preferentially in vascular smooth muscle, reducing calcium influx and causing arteriolar vasodilation
records.effect: Peripheral arterial vasodilation, reduced systemic vascular resistance and blood pressure

records.mechanism_of_action: Activates peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor alpha (PPAR-alpha), increasing lipoprotein lipase activity and fatty-acid oxidation while reducing apolipoprotein C-III, thereby lowering VLDL and triglycerides and raising HDL-C
records.effect: Reduced serum triglycerides and VLDL, modest LDL lowering, increased HDL-C

records.mechanism_of_action: Competitive inhibition of type II 5-alpha-reductase, reducing conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT)
records.effect: Lowered serum and intraprostatic DHT, reduced prostate volume and slowed hair-follicle miniaturisation

records.mechanism_of_action: High-affinity glucocorticoid receptor agonist; binds the intracellular receptor to modulate transcription of anti-inflammatory and pro-inflammatory genes, reducing airway inflammatory cell infiltration and mediator release
records.effect: reduced airway inflammation and bronchial hyper-responsiveness

records.mechanism_of_action: Long-acting competitive antagonist of muscarinic (predominantly M3) receptors on airway smooth muscle, inhibiting acetylcholine-mediated bronchoconstriction
records.effect: Sustained bronchodilation

records.mechanism_of_action: Long-acting selective beta2-adrenoceptor agonist that stimulates adenylate cyclase, raising cyclic AMP and relaxing airway smooth muscle
records.effect: Sustained bronchodilation

records.mechanism_of_action: Short-acting weak oestrogen that binds nuclear oestrogen receptors, restoring vaginal/urogenital epithelium, pH and blood flow with relatively low proliferative effect on endometrium
records.effect: Reversal of urogenital atrophy

records.mechanism_of_action: Reduces low-threshold T-type calcium currents in thalamocortical relay neurons, suppressing the 3-Hz spike-wave oscillations that generate absence seizures
records.effect: Suppression of absence seizure activity

records.mechanism_of_action: Binds FKBP-12 to form a complex that inhibits mTOR complex 1 (mTORC1), reducing cell proliferation, angiogenesis (via VEGF/HIF-1) and glucose uptake, and blunting T- and B-cell activation
records.effect: Antiproliferative, antiangiogenic and immunosuppressive

records.mechanism_of_action: Irreversible steroidal (suicide) inhibitor of aromatase (CYP19) that blocks peripheral conversion of androgens to oestrogens, lowering circulating oestrogen in postmenopausal women
records.effect: Oestrogen deprivation

records.mechanism_of_action: Potent glucocorticoid receptor agonist; binds the intracellular receptor to alter gene transcription, suppressing multiple inflammatory pathways in the airway
records.effect: reduced airway inflammation and mucosal oedema

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective long-acting agonist at beta-2 adrenoceptors; a lipophilic side chain anchors it at an exosite giving prolonged receptor activation, stimulating adenylate cyclase and raising cyclic AMP to relax airway smooth muscle
records.effect: Sustained bronchodilation

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor; blocks the presynaptic serotonin transporter (SERT) to increase synaptic serotonin; also a sigma-1 receptor agonist
records.effect: enhanced serotonergic neurotransmission

records.mechanism_of_action: Activates peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor alpha (PPAR-alpha), increasing lipoprotein lipase activity and reducing hepatic VLDL production
records.effect: Lowers serum triglycerides and raises HDL cholesterol

records.mechanism_of_action: Binds the SUR1 subunit of the ATP-sensitive potassium channel on pancreatic beta cells, closing the channel to depolarise the cell and trigger insulin release
records.target: SUR1 subunit of pancreatic beta-cell K-ATP channel
records.effect: Stimulates endogenous insulin secretion, lowering blood glucose

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective inhibition of the presynaptic serotonin transporter, increasing synaptic serotonin availability
records.effect: Increased serotonergic neurotransmission

records.mechanism_of_action: Irreversible inhibition of the gastric H+/K+-ATPase (proton pump) in parietal cells, blocking the final step of acid secretion
records.effect: Marked, sustained suppression of gastric acid secretion

records.mechanism_of_action: Agonist at nuclear estrogen receptors (ERalpha/ERbeta), modulating gene transcription in estrogen-responsive tissues
records.effect: Replacement of endogenous estrogenic activity on reproductive, bone and vasomotor tissue

records.mechanism_of_action: Agonist at the cytoplasmic glucocorticoid receptor, altering gene transcription to suppress pro-inflammatory mediators and immune cell function; also activates the mineralocorticoid receptor
records.effect: Anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive effects, sodium/water retention, and physiological glucocorticoid replacement

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective long-acting beta2-adrenoceptor agonist; stimulates adenylate cyclase, raising intracellular cyclic AMP, which relaxes airway smooth muscle and produces sustained bronchodilation.
records.effect: Sustained bronchodilation (once-daily)

records.mechanism_of_action: Synthetic glucocorticoid; binds intracellular glucocorticoid receptors to modulate transcription of pro-inflammatory genes, reducing airway inflammation, mucosal oedema and bronchial hyper-responsiveness.
records.effect: Local airway anti-inflammatory effect

records.mechanism_of_action: Thiazide-like diuretic that inhibits the sodium-chloride cotransporter in the distal convoluted tubule, promoting natriuresis; also exerts a direct vascular smooth-muscle relaxant effect
records.effect: Natriuresis, reduced plasma volume and peripheral resistance, lowered blood pressure

records.mechanism_of_action: Binds the SUR1 subunit of ATP-sensitive potassium (K-ATP) channels on pancreatic beta cells, closing them; the resulting membrane depolarisation opens voltage-gated calcium channels, increasing intracellular calcium and stimulating insulin secretion. Effect is glucose-independent (insulin secretion occurs regardless of glucose level).
records.effect: Increased endogenous insulin secretion; lowered blood glucose

records.mechanism_of_action: Binds the SUR1 subunit of pancreatic beta-cell ATP-sensitive potassium channels, closing them and causing depolarisation, calcium influx and insulin release; augments insulin secretion in a glucose-independent manner.
records.effect: Increased insulin secretion; lowered blood glucose

records.mechanism_of_action: High-molecular-weight glycosaminoglycan that is a natural constituent of synovial fluid and extracellular matrix. Restores the viscoelasticity and lubricating/shock-absorbing properties of synovial fluid (viscosupplementation) and may exert secondary anti-inflammatory and chondroprotective effects; on the ocular surface it retains moisture and lubricates. Action is largely physical/mechanical rather than receptor-mediated.
records.effect: Improved joint lubrication and cushioning; ocular surface lubrication

records.mechanism_of_action: Blocks voltage-gated sodium channels, stabilising presynaptic neuronal membranes and inhibiting release of excitatory neurotransmitters, principally glutamate
records.effect: reduced neuronal excitability and seizure propagation; mood stabilisation

records.mechanism_of_action: Acid-activated prodrug that irreversibly inhibits the gastric H+/K+-ATPase (proton pump) on the luminal surface of parietal cells
records.effect: suppression of basal and stimulated gastric acid secretion

records.mechanism_of_action: Trivalent lanthanum cation binds dietary phosphate in the gastrointestinal tract forming insoluble lanthanum phosphate, reducing phosphate absorption
records.target: dietary phosphate in the gut lumen
records.effect: reduction of serum phosphate

records.mechanism_of_action: Prostaglandin F2-alpha analogue that agonises the FP prostanoid receptor, increasing uveoscleral (and trabecular) outflow of aqueous humour
records.effect: reduction of intraocular pressure

records.mechanism_of_action: Organic nitrate prodrug; denitrated to release nitric oxide, which activates soluble guanylate cyclase, raising intracellular cGMP and causing vascular smooth muscle relaxation. Predominant venodilation reduces preload; also modest arterial and coronary vasodilation.
records.effect: Venodilation with reduced preload, reduced myocardial oxygen demand, coronary vasodilation

records.mechanism_of_action: Active mononitrate metabolite of isosorbide dinitrate; releases nitric oxide which activates soluble guanylate cyclase, increasing cGMP and relaxing vascular smooth muscle with predominant venodilation and preload reduction.
records.effect: Venodilation, reduced preload and myocardial oxygen demand

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective, specific inhibition of the funny (If) current in sinoatrial node pacemaker cells, slowing diastolic depolarisation and reducing heart rate without affecting myocardial contractility, ventricular repolarisation or blood pressure.
records.target: HCN (hyperpolarisation-activated cyclic nucleotide-gated) channels of the sinoatrial node
records.effect: Pure heart-rate reduction in sinus rhythm

records.mechanism_of_action: Competitive antagonism of both beta-adrenoceptors (non-selective beta1/beta2) and alpha1-adrenoceptors; alpha1 blockade reduces peripheral vascular resistance while beta blockade limits reflex tachycardia. Alpha:beta blocking ratio approximately 1:3 oral and 1:7 intravenous.
records.effect: Lowered blood pressure via reduced peripheral resistance without marked reflex tachycardia

records.mechanism_of_action: Selectively enhances slow inactivation of voltage-gated sodium channels, stabilising hyperexcitable neuronal membranes and limiting sustained repetitive firing; also interacts with collapsin response mediator protein-2 (CRMP-2), significance uncertain.
records.effect: Reduced neuronal hyperexcitability and seizure activity

records.mechanism_of_action: Peripheral aromatic L-amino acid (DOPA) decarboxylase inhibitor that does not cross the blood-brain barrier; blocks peripheral conversion of levodopa to dopamine, increasing the fraction of levodopa reaching the CNS and reducing peripheral dopaminergic effects.
records.effect: Increases central levodopa availability and lowers effective levodopa dose and peripheral adverse effects

records.mechanism_of_action: Peripheral aromatic L-amino acid (DOPA) decarboxylase inhibitor that does not cross the blood-brain barrier; reduces peripheral decarboxylation of levodopa, increasing central delivery.
records.effect: Increases central levodopa availability and reduces peripheral nausea/hypotension

records.mechanism_of_action: Prodrug converted to the active metabolite teriflunomide, which reversibly inhibits dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (DHODH), the rate-limiting enzyme of de novo pyrimidine synthesis, arresting proliferation of activated autoimmune lymphocytes
records.effect: Antiproliferative/immunomodulatory reduction of activated T- and B-lymphocyte expansion

records.mechanism_of_action: Blocks L-type voltage-gated calcium channels in vascular smooth muscle, reducing transmembrane calcium influx and producing peripheral arterial vasodilation
records.effect: Reduced peripheral vascular resistance and lowered blood pressure

records.mechanism_of_action: Reversibly and selectively inhibits the aromatase enzyme (CYP19A1), blocking peripheral conversion of androgens to oestrogens and markedly lowering circulating oestrogen in postmenopausal women
records.effect: Oestrogen deprivation of hormone-sensitive breast tumour tissue

records.mechanism_of_action: Binds synaptic vesicle glycoprotein 2A (SV2A), modulating neurotransmitter vesicle release and reducing hypersynchronous epileptiform neuronal firing; classic ion-channel and receptor mechanisms are not the principal action
records.effect: Reduced seizure propagation

records.mechanism_of_action: Central presynaptic alpha-2 adrenoceptor antagonist that enhances noradrenergic transmission; also antagonises 5-HT2 and histamine H1 receptors
records.effect: Antidepressant and sedative

records.mechanism_of_action: Binds the bacterial 30S ribosomal subunit and blocks aminoacyl-tRNA binding, inhibiting protein synthesis
records.effect: Bacteriostatic; anti-inflammatory in acne

records.mechanism_of_action: Prodrug converted to minoxidil sulfate, which opens ATP-sensitive potassium channels in arteriolar smooth muscle causing membrane hyperpolarisation and vasodilation
records.effect: Peripheral arterial vasodilation; stimulation of hair follicle growth

records.mechanism_of_action: Antagonist at central presynaptic alpha-2 adrenoceptors increasing noradrenergic and serotonergic transmission; blocks postsynaptic 5-HT2, 5-HT3 and H1 receptors
records.effect: Antidepressant, sedative and appetite-stimulant

records.mechanism_of_action: Reversible selective inhibitor of monoamine oxidase type A (RIMA), reducing metabolism of noradrenaline, serotonin and dopamine
records.effect: Antidepressant; increased central monoamine availability

records.mechanism_of_action: Synthetic triiodothyronine (T3) that binds nuclear thyroid hormone receptors and regulates gene transcription, increasing basal metabolic rate, oxygen consumption, and cardiac, CNS and metabolic activity
records.effect: Restoration of euthyroid metabolic state; faster onset than levothyroxine

records.mechanism_of_action: Inhibits angiotensin-converting enzyme (kininase II), reducing conversion of angiotensin I to angiotensin II and decreasing aldosterone secretion, while reducing bradykinin degradation
records.effect: Vasodilation, reduced blood pressure and afterload, natriuresis, renoprotection

records.mechanism_of_action: High-molecular-weight, non-absorbed polyethylene glycol polymer that binds water osmotically within the intestinal lumen, increasing stool water content and volume and promoting peristalsis
records.effect: Stool softening and increased frequency of bowel movements

records.mechanism_of_action: Synthetic progestogen that binds progesterone receptors to induce secretory endometrial transformation and suppresses hypothalamic-pituitary gonadotrophin (LH/FSH) release, inhibiting ovulation; has weak androgenic and glucocorticoid activity
records.effect: Ovulation suppression, endometrial stabilisation, anti-oestrogenic effect on hormone-dependent tissue

records.mechanism_of_action: Locally acting aminosalicylate (5-aminosalicylic acid) that reduces colonic mucosal inflammation by inhibiting cyclo-oxygenase and lipoxygenase prostaglandin/leukotriene synthesis, scavenging reactive oxygen species, and activating PPAR-gamma / inhibiting NF-kappaB signalling
records.effect: Reduced intestinal mucosal inflammation

records.mechanism_of_action: In acidic urine methenamine is hydrolysed to formaldehyde, a non-specific bactericidal agent that denatures bacterial proteins and nucleic acids; the hippurate moiety assists urinary acidification.
records.effect: urinary bactericidal action; prevents bacteriuria (no systemic antibacterial effect)

records.mechanism_of_action: Competitively inhibits dihydrofolate reductase, depleting reduced folates and impairing thymidylate and purine synthesis; at low doses promotes adenosine release (via AICAR transformylase inhibition) producing an anti-inflammatory effect.
records.effect: antiproliferative, immunosuppressive and anti-inflammatory

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective competitive antagonism of beta-1 adrenoceptors, reducing heart rate, contractility, AV nodal conduction and renin release.
records.effect: negative chronotropic/inotropic/dromotropic; lowers blood pressure and myocardial oxygen demand

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective competitive antagonism of beta-1 adrenoceptors, reducing heart rate, contractility, AV nodal conduction and renin release.
records.effect: negative chronotropic/inotropic/dromotropic; lowers blood pressure and myocardial oxygen demand

records.mechanism_of_action: Blocks L-type voltage-gated calcium channels in vascular smooth muscle, inhibiting calcium influx and producing arterial vasodilation with reduced peripheral resistance
records.effect: Peripheral and coronary arterial vasodilation, reduced afterload and blood pressure

records.mechanism_of_action: Competitively and reversibly antagonises histamine H2 receptors on gastric parietal cells, reducing basal, nocturnal and stimulated gastric acid secretion
records.effect: Reduced gastric acid output and raised intragastric pH

records.mechanism_of_action: Synthetic progestogen that agonises the progesterone receptor, inducing secretory then atrophic endometrial change, suppressing endometrial proliferation and (at contraceptive doses) inhibiting ovulation and thickening cervical mucus
records.effect: Endometrial stabilisation/atrophy, menstrual control, contraceptive effect

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective competitive antagonist of the angiotensin II type 1 (AT1) receptor, blocking angiotensin II-mediated vasoconstriction and aldosterone secretion
records.effect: Vasodilation, reduced aldosterone, lowered blood pressure

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective, reversible antagonist of the cysteinyl leukotriene CysLT1 receptor, blocking the actions of LTC4/LTD4/LTE4 and thereby reducing leukotriene-mediated bronchoconstriction, airway oedema and inflammatory cell recruitment
records.effect: Bronchodilation and reduced airway inflammation; attenuation of allergen- and exercise-induced responses

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective agonist at imidazoline I1 receptors (with lesser central alpha-2 adrenoceptor activity) in the rostral ventrolateral medulla, reducing central sympathetic outflow
records.drug_class: Centrally acting antihypertensive (imidazoline I1 receptor agonist)
records.effect: Reduced sympathetic tone, lowered peripheral vascular resistance and blood pressure

records.mechanism_of_action: Mycophenolic acid is a potent, selective, reversible non-competitive inhibitor of inosine-5'-monophosphate dehydrogenase (IMPDH), blocking de novo guanosine nucleotide synthesis on which proliferating T and B lymphocytes are dependent
records.effect: Suppression of T- and B-lymphocyte proliferation and antibody production

records.mechanism_of_action: Highly selective beta-1 adrenoceptor antagonist that additionally promotes endothelial nitric oxide release, producing peripheral vasodilation
records.drug_class: Beta-1 selective adrenoceptor blocker with nitric-oxide-mediated vasodilator activity
records.target: Beta-1 adrenoceptor; endothelial nitric oxide pathway
records.effect: Reduced heart rate, myocardial contractility and blood pressure with vasodilation

records.mechanism_of_action: Dual-action antianginal: activates ATP-sensitive potassium (K-ATP) channels causing arterial and coronary vasodilation, and possesses a nitrate moiety that releases nitric oxide to stimulate guanylate cyclase and venodilate
records.target: ATP-sensitive potassium (K-ATP) channels; soluble guanylate cyclase (via NO)
records.effect: Coronary and systemic arterial vasodilation with venodilation, reducing preload and afterload and improving myocardial oxygen balance

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective non-competitive antagonist of the AMPA-type ionotropic glutamate receptor on post-synaptic neurons, reducing glutamate-mediated excitatory neurotransmission
records.effect: Reduced neuronal hyperexcitability and seizure propagation

records.mechanism_of_action: Prodrug hydrolysed to perindoprilat, which inhibits angiotensin-converting enzyme, blocking conversion of angiotensin I to angiotensin II and reducing aldosterone secretion; also decreases bradykinin degradation
records.effect: Vasodilation, reduced systemic vascular resistance and blood pressure, reduced cardiac afterload

records.mechanism_of_action: Beta-lactam that binds penicillin-binding proteins and inhibits transpeptidation (cross-linking) of peptidoglycan in the bacterial cell wall, producing bactericidal cell lysis in dividing organisms
records.effect: Bactericidal against susceptible gram-positive organisms, especially streptococci

records.mechanism_of_action: Provides the potassium cation to restore and maintain intracellular and extracellular potassium, the principal determinant of resting membrane potential and normal neuromuscular and cardiac conduction
records.effect: Corrects hypokalaemia and normalises membrane excitability

records.mechanism_of_action: Non-ergot dopamine agonist that directly stimulates dopamine receptors, with preferential affinity for the D3 subtype of the D2 receptor family, restoring dopaminergic neurotransmission in the striatum
records.effect: Improves parkinsonian motor symptoms and restless legs symptoms

records.mechanism_of_action: Competitively inhibits HMG-CoA reductase, the rate-limiting enzyme of hepatic cholesterol synthesis, upregulating LDL receptors and increasing clearance of circulating LDL cholesterol
records.effect: Lowers LDL cholesterol and total cholesterol; modest triglyceride reduction and HDL increase

records.mechanism_of_action: Glucocorticoid that binds the intracellular glucocorticoid receptor and modulates gene transcription, suppressing pro-inflammatory mediators and immune cell function while exerting mild mineralocorticoid activity
records.effect: Broad anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive effect

records.mechanism_of_action: Replenishes body phosphate stores essential for ATP and 2,3-diphosphoglycerate synthesis, cell-membrane phospholipids and bone mineralisation, correcting deficiency states
records.effect: corrects hypophosphataemia

records.mechanism_of_action: Directly stimulates muscarinic acetylcholine receptors (parasympathomimetic), increasing exocrine gland secretion and producing smooth-muscle contraction
records.effect: increased salivary and sweat secretion; miosis and ciliary contraction (ophthalmic)

records.mechanism_of_action: Agonist at peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor gamma (PPAR-γ), modulating transcription of insulin-responsive genes to enhance peripheral glucose uptake and reduce hepatic glucose output
records.effect: improved insulin sensitivity and lower blood glucose

records.mechanism_of_action: Antagonises serotonin 5-HT2 receptors with additional H1-antihistaminic and anticholinergic activity, stabilising cranial vascular tone for migraine prophylaxis
records.effect: reduced frequency of migraine attacks

records.mechanism_of_action: Irreversibly inhibits the gastric H+/K+-ATPase (proton pump) in parietal cells after acid activation, blocking the final step of gastric acid secretion
records.effect: Sustained suppression of basal and stimulated gastric acid secretion

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective, reversible peripheral catechol-O-methyltransferase (COMT) inhibitor that reduces peripheral metabolism of levodopa, increasing and prolonging levodopa availability to the brain
records.effect: Prolonged levodopa plasma exposure; increased motor on-time

records.mechanism_of_action: Blockade of voltage-gated sodium channels (largely via the active 10-monohydroxy metabolite), stabilising hyperexcited neuronal membranes and inhibiting repetitive firing
records.effect: Reduced neuronal excitability and seizure propagation

records.mechanism_of_action: Competitive antagonism of muscarinic (predominantly M3) acetylcholine receptors on detrusor smooth muscle, reducing involuntary detrusor contractions; also has direct smooth-muscle antispasmodic and local anaesthetic activity
records.effect: Reduced detrusor overactivity and increased bladder capacity

records.mechanism_of_action: Provides exogenous porcine pancreatic enzymes (lipase, amylase, protease) that catalyse hydrolysis of dietary fats, starches and proteins in the duodenum, substituting for deficient endogenous secretion
records.effect: Improved nutrient digestion and absorption; reduced steatorrhoea

records.mechanism_of_action: Potent selective inhibition of the presynaptic serotonin transporter (SERT), increasing synaptic 5-HT availability; also has weak noradrenaline reuptake inhibition and mild antimuscarinic activity
records.effect: Enhanced serotonergic neurotransmission

records.mechanism_of_action: Chelates copper and other heavy metals promoting their urinary excretion; in cystinuria forms a soluble penicillamine-cysteine mixed disulfide; in rheumatoid arthritis exerts poorly understood immunomodulatory effects (reduces rheumatoid factor and T-cell activity)
records.effect: Increased urinary excretion of copper; reduced cystine stone formation; immunomodulation

records.mechanism_of_action: Prodrug converted to prednisolone, which binds the cytoplasmic glucocorticoid receptor; the complex modulates transcription to suppress pro-inflammatory cytokines and mediators
records.effect: Anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive

records.mechanism_of_action: Inhibits the renal proximal tubular urate transporter URAT1, reducing tubular reabsorption of uric acid and increasing its urinary excretion; also inhibits organic anion transporters (OAT) reducing tubular secretion of many anionic drugs
records.effect: Increased renal uric acid excretion; reduced renal clearance of co-administered organic anions

records.mechanism_of_action: Agonist at the nuclear progesterone receptor, inducing secretory transformation of oestrogen-primed endometrium and opposing oestrogen-driven endometrial proliferation
records.effect: Endometrial secretory transformation and endometrial protection; luteal support

records.mechanism_of_action: Quaternary ammonium competitive antagonist of acetylcholine at muscarinic receptors, reducing gastrointestinal and urinary smooth-muscle tone and exocrine secretions
records.effect: Reduced GI motility and secretion; antispasmodic

records.mechanism_of_action: Inhibits thyroid peroxidase, blocking iodination and coupling of tyrosine residues on thyroglobulin; also inhibits peripheral 5'-deiodinase conversion of T4 to active T3
records.effect: Reduced thyroid hormone synthesis and reduced peripheral T3 generation

records.mechanism_of_action: Reversibly inhibits acetylcholinesterase, reducing hydrolysis of acetylcholine and increasing its concentration at neuromuscular and autonomic synapses
records.effect: Enhanced cholinergic neuromuscular transmission

records.mechanism_of_action: Prodrug activated in the acidic parietal-cell canaliculus; covalently and irreversibly inhibits the gastric H+/K+-ATPase (proton pump)
records.effect: Marked, prolonged suppression of gastric acid secretion

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective oestrogen receptor modulator with tissue-selective agonist activity in bone and antagonist activity in breast and endometrium
records.effect: Decreased bone resorption and increased bone mineral density without endometrial/breast stimulation

records.mechanism_of_action: Irreversible selective inhibition of monoamine oxidase type B, reducing central dopamine metabolism and increasing striatal dopaminergic activity
records.effect: Increased and prolonged dopaminergic neurotransmission

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective inhibition of the noradrenaline (norepinephrine) transporter, blocking presynaptic reuptake and increasing synaptic noradrenaline
records.effect: Increased noradrenergic neurotransmission with antidepressant effect

records.mechanism_of_action: Inhibits glutamate release, inactivates voltage-dependent sodium channels and interferes with NMDA receptor-mediated events, reducing glutamate-mediated excitotoxicity
records.target: Voltage-gated sodium channels and glutamatergic neurotransmission
records.effect: Reduced excitotoxic motor neurone injury; modest prolongation of survival in ALS

records.mechanism_of_action: Nitrogen-containing bisphosphonate that binds bone hydroxyapatite and inhibits farnesyl pyrophosphate synthase in the mevalonate pathway of osteoclasts, impairing osteoclast function and inducing apoptosis
records.effect: Reduced osteoclast-mediated bone resorption, decreased bone turnover and increased bone mineral density

records.mechanism_of_action: Competitive inhibition of HMG-CoA reductase, reducing hepatic cholesterol synthesis and up-regulating hepatic LDL receptors to increase LDL clearance
records.effect: Lowering of LDL-cholesterol and total cholesterol

records.mechanism_of_action: Non-ergot dopamine receptor agonist with activity across D3, D2 and D1 receptors, providing continuous dopaminergic stimulation via transdermal delivery
records.effect: Dopaminergic receptor stimulation

records.mechanism_of_action: Prodrug converted to sacubitrilat, which inhibits neprilysin, reducing degradation of natriuretic peptides (ANP, BNP), bradykinin and other vasoactive peptides
records.effect: Increased natriuretic peptide activity, natriuresis and vasodilation

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective, reversible inhibition of monoamine oxidase-B increasing striatal dopamine; also state-dependent blockade of voltage-gated sodium channels and inhibition of stimulated glutamate release
records.effect: Increased dopaminergic tone and reduced excitatory glutamate release

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective inhibition of presynaptic serotonin (5-HT) reuptake at the serotonin transporter, increasing synaptic serotonin availability
records.effect: Antidepressant and anxiolytic effect via enhanced serotonergic neurotransmission

records.mechanism_of_action: Non-absorbed cationic polymer that binds dietary phosphate ions in the gastrointestinal lumen, reducing phosphate absorption
records.target: Dietary phosphate in the gut lumen
records.effect: Lowering of serum phosphate concentration

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective antagonism of alpha-1A adrenoceptors in prostatic and bladder-neck smooth muscle, reducing smooth-muscle tone
records.effect: Relief of lower urinary tract obstruction and improved urinary flow in BPH

records.mechanism_of_action: Binds the immunophilin FKBP-12; the complex inhibits mammalian target of rapamycin (mTOR), arresting cytokine-driven T-lymphocyte proliferation at the G1-to-S phase transition
records.effect: Suppression of T-cell proliferation and immune response

records.mechanism_of_action: Competitive antagonism of the mineralocorticoid (aldosterone) receptor in the distal renal tubule, promoting sodium and water excretion while conserving potassium; also antagonises androgen receptors and inhibits androgen synthesis
records.drug_class: Aldosterone antagonist / potassium-sparing diuretic (mineralocorticoid receptor antagonist)
records.effect: Natriuresis with potassium retention, blood pressure reduction, cardiac remodelling benefit, and anti-androgenic effect

records.mechanism_of_action: Iron(III)-based binder that adsorbs dietary phosphate in the gastrointestinal lumen by ligand exchange between surface hydroxyl groups/water and phosphate ions, forming an insoluble complex excreted in faeces; iron is minimally absorbed
records.effect: Reduces phosphate absorption, lowering serum phosphate

records.mechanism_of_action: Prodrug cleaved by colonic bacteria into sulfapyridine and 5-aminosalicylic acid (mesalazine); 5-ASA acts topically in the bowel to inhibit prostaglandin/leukotriene synthesis and NF-kB-mediated inflammation, while the sulfapyridine moiety mediates the systemic immunomodulatory/DMARD effect in inflammatory arthritis (exact mechanism incompletely defined)
records.effect: Anti-inflammatory action in inflammatory bowel disease and disease modification in inflammatory arthritis

records.mechanism_of_action: Reversibly inhibits vesicular monoamine transporter type 2 (VMAT2), depleting presynaptic storage of dopamine (and to a lesser extent serotonin and noradrenaline) in the CNS, reducing dopaminergic transmission
records.effect: Reduction of hyperkinetic involuntary movements (chorea)

records.mechanism_of_action: Converted to the active coenzyme thiamine pyrophosphate, an essential cofactor for pyruvate dehydrogenase, alpha-ketoglutarate dehydrogenase and transketolase in carbohydrate metabolism and the pentose phosphate pathway
records.effect: Restoration of normal carbohydrate and energy metabolism

records.mechanism_of_action: Selectively inhibits the GAT-1 GABA transporter, blocking neuronal and glial reuptake of GABA and thereby increasing GABA availability at synapses to enhance inhibitory neurotransmission
records.effect: Enhanced GABAergic inhibition and raised seizure threshold

records.mechanism_of_action: Reversibly and non-competitively binds the platelet P2Y12 ADP receptor at a site distinct from the ADP binding site, inhibiting ADP-mediated platelet activation and aggregation without requiring metabolic activation
records.effect: Inhibition of platelet aggregation

records.mechanism_of_action: Inhibits carbonic anhydrase and modulates neuronal voltage-gated sodium currents, raising seizure threshold
records.target: Carbonic anhydrase isoenzymes; neuronal sodium channels
records.effect: Anticonvulsant

records.mechanism_of_action: Binds FKBP-12 to form a complex that inhibits calcineurin phosphatase, blocking NFAT-dependent transcription of IL-2 and other cytokines, suppressing T-lymphocyte activation
records.effect: Immunosuppression

records.mechanism_of_action: Competitively binds the oestrogen receptor acting as antagonist in breast tissue (agonist in endometrium and bone); active largely via CYP2D6-derived metabolite endoxifen
records.effect: Anti-oestrogenic (breast); growth inhibition of ER-positive tumour cells

records.mechanism_of_action: Selectively blocks angiotensin II binding at the AT1 receptor, producing vasodilation and reduced aldosterone secretion; also partial PPAR-gamma agonist
records.effect: Antihypertensive

records.mechanism_of_action: Prodrug converted intracellularly to tenofovir diphosphate, a nucleotide analogue that competitively inhibits HIV reverse transcriptase (and HBV polymerase) and causes DNA chain termination
records.target: HIV-1 reverse transcriptase / HBV DNA polymerase
records.effect: Suppression of viral replication

records.mechanism_of_action: Cytidine analogue phosphorylated intracellularly to emtricitabine triphosphate, which competitively inhibits HIV reverse transcriptase and terminates the growing DNA chain
records.effect: Suppression of viral replication

records.mechanism_of_action: Recombinant human parathyroid hormone fragment (1-34) that binds the PTH1 receptor; intermittent daily exposure preferentially stimulates osteoblastic bone formation over resorption
records.effect: Increased bone formation, bone mineral density and bone strength

records.mechanism_of_action: Endogenous androgen that binds and activates the androgen receptor; also aromatised to estradiol and 5-alpha-reduced to dihydrotestosterone to exert downstream androgenic and anabolic effects
records.effect: Development and maintenance of male secondary sexual characteristics, anabolic and erythropoietic effects

records.mechanism_of_action: Reversible competitive inhibition of dipeptidyl peptidase-4 (DPP-4), preventing degradation of the incretin hormones GLP-1 and GIP, which enhances glucose-dependent insulin secretion and suppresses glucagon release
records.effect: Glucose-dependent lowering of blood glucose

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective irreversible inhibition of monoamine oxidase type B at low doses, reducing central dopamine catabolism; loses B-selectivity and also inhibits MAO-A at higher doses
records.effect: Increased striatal dopamine availability

records.mechanism_of_action: Agonist at the glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) receptor; augments glucose-dependent insulin secretion, suppresses glucagon, slows gastric emptying and increases satiety centrally
records.effect: Glucose lowering, reduced appetite and weight

records.mechanism_of_action: Prodrug hydrolysed to active trandolaprilat, which inhibits angiotensin-converting enzyme, reducing angiotensin II and aldosterone production and decreasing bradykinin degradation
records.effect: Vasodilation, reduced peripheral resistance and blood pressure, reduced aldosterone-mediated sodium/water retention

records.mechanism_of_action: Irreversible non-selective inhibitor of monoamine oxidase A and B, reducing catabolism of noradrenaline, serotonin and dopamine and increasing their synaptic availability
records.effect: Increased central monoaminergic neurotransmission; antidepressant effect

records.mechanism_of_action: Prostaglandin F2-alpha analogue; the free-acid metabolite is a selective FP prostanoid receptor agonist that increases uveoscleral (and trabecular) outflow of aqueous humour
records.effect: Increased aqueous outflow and lowered intraocular pressure

records.mechanism_of_action: Long-acting competitive antagonist of muscarinic receptors on airway smooth muscle, with slow dissociation from M3 receptors producing prolonged bronchodilation
records.effect: Bronchodilation and reduced bronchoconstrictor tone

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective long-acting beta2-adrenoceptor agonist that stimulates adenylate cyclase, raising intracellular cyclic AMP and relaxing bronchial smooth muscle
records.effect: Bronchodilation

records.mechanism_of_action: Binds the bacterial 30S ribosomal subunit, causing misreading of mRNA and inhibition of protein synthesis; concentration-dependent bactericidal activity
records.effect: Bactericidal against aerobic Gram-negative bacilli including Pseudomonas aeruginosa

records.mechanism_of_action: Blocks voltage-gated sodium channels, potentiates GABA-A receptor activity, antagonises AMPA/kainate glutamate receptors, and weakly inhibits carbonic anhydrase, collectively reducing neuronal excitability
records.target: Voltage-gated sodium channels, GABA-A receptors, AMPA/kainate glutamate receptors, carbonic anhydrase
records.effect: Reduced neuronal hyperexcitability and elevated seizure threshold

records.mechanism_of_action: Inhibits serotonin and noradrenaline reuptake (dose-dependent, serotonergic at low dose with added noradrenergic effect at higher dose); weak dopamine reuptake inhibition
records.effect: Increased synaptic serotonin and noradrenaline

records.mechanism_of_action: Irreversibly inhibits GABA-transaminase (GABA-T), the enzyme that catabolises GABA, thereby increasing central GABA concentrations and enhancing inhibitory neurotransmission
records.effect: Increased brain GABA and raised seizure threshold

records.mechanism_of_action: Selectively and reversibly inhibits dipeptidyl peptidase-4 (DPP-4), reducing degradation of incretin hormones (GLP-1, GIP), which enhances glucose-dependent insulin secretion and suppresses glucagon release
records.effect: Glucose-dependent increase in insulin and decrease in glucagon, lowering fasting and postprandial glucose

records.mechanism_of_action: Blocks voltage-gated sodium channels and T-type calcium channels to stabilise neuronal membranes and suppress repetitive firing; also has weak carbonic anhydrase inhibitory activity
records.target: Voltage-gated sodium channels and T-type calcium channels
records.effect: Suppression of seizure spread and neuronal hyperexcitability
